_comment: Gate-set bundles for `haggis-eval slice <name>`. Each bundle is an ordered list of gate IDs that map to the wired subcommands in tools/haggis-eval/internal/cmd. Use these to run an opinionated subset without typing the gate matrix every time. Filename note: the kernel design spec calls this slices.toml; this project's haggis-eval CLI is stdlib-only Go, so JSON is the right call (encoding/json is stdlib; TOML is not). The schema field versions the config so future shape changes can be migrated without surprise.

pre-merge.description: Fast pre-merge gate-set — what a contributor runs before opening a PR. Skips rust/rust-cov, coverage, multi-browser, soak, supply-chain, and the differential tests — all release-gate territory. Keeps typecheck + vitest + build + security headers + perf budgets (both halves) + five chromium browser smokes + determinism + visual + a11y. Closest analogue to `pnpm verify` but extended with the browser tier.

release.description: Full release-gate matrix. Equivalent to `haggis-eval all` but invoked through the slice mechanism so a release script can name the bundle explicitly. Useful when you want the signed JSON report generated — note: only `all` writes the report; `slice release` runs the same gates but does NOT sign a report.

fast.description: Smoke-only — the absolute minimum to confirm the toolchain still works. Typecheck, vitest, build, bundle-size + paint-timing budgets. ~10 seconds end to end. Use when iterating on docs or non-rendered code paths.
